title: Define and execute MeshCore app/client smoke-test script
description: Why this exists: automated tests do not prove real MeshCore client compatibility. What needs to be done: write the manual/automated client smoke-test procedure for the relevant MeshCore app or tool against LICHEN firmware, including firmware build/flash command, transport discovery/connect steps, expected identity/status/config screens or CLI output, send-message action, injected incoming-message/status action, and log capture. Execute when target hardware or simulator/client access is available. Acceptance criteria: client/app version/date/device recorded; firmware logs and client observations captured in Beads notes; failures create exact blocker Beads with logs.
notes:
Input from project-LICHEN-9c4y.15 review: MeshCore app smoke testing must include BLE coexistence-specific cases: advertised name/service discovery in MeshCore mode only, encrypted MITM/PIN pairing behavior, MTU 185 negotiation plus default-ATT fallback/limits, RX write and TX notify raw-frame behavior, rejection of serial/TCP 0x3c/0x3e length-prefixed frames on BLE, and wrong-mode failure symptoms for native NUS/SLIP and Meshtastic clients.
Follow-up from project-LICHEN-9c4y.15 review: verify MeshCore-LICHEN discovery as the default advertised name, or capture evidence for any alternate prefix before allowing it. Smoke procedure should also confirm production GATT permissions require encrypted authenticated pairing/passkey and that plain permissions are limited to test-only builds.
